operating_state: Total revenue increased 12% to $5.3 billion driven by organic base fee growth, market beta impact on AUM, and GIP Transaction contributions, partially offset by performance fees declining to $60 million from $204 million due to lower alternatives performance. Investment advisory fees rose while technology services revenue increased reflecting sustained Aladdin demand and completed Preqin acquisition. Operating activities generated $2.99 billion in cash flow compared to $876 million in the prior year period.
cost_structure: Total expenses increased 18% to $3.6 billion driven by acquisition-related costs from GIP and Preqin transactions, with employee compensation rising including nonrecurring retention costs. General administration expenses increased primarily from transaction costs and contingent consideration fair value adjustments. Amortization of intangible assets rose significantly reflecting recent acquisitions, with operating margin as adjusted improving to 43.2% from 42.2% despite GAAP margin compression.
material_events: Completed acquisition of Preqin for $3.2 billion in March 2025, adding private markets data capabilities and recognizing $2.3 billion in goodwill and $1.2 billion in intangible assets. Announced definitive agreement to acquire HPS Investment Partners for up to 13.7 million BlackRock shares, expected to close mid-2025 subject to regulatory approvals. Repurchased $657 million in shares and paid $838 million in dividends during the quarter, with net cash decreasing $5.0 billion primarily due to Preqin acquisition funding.
macro_exposures: AUM increased from foreign exchange movements due to US dollar weakening against major currencies, while net market depreciation was driven by US equity market decline. The company maintains derivative positions through exchange-traded futures to hedge seed investment exposures and foreign currency forward contracts. Economic investment exposure totals $4.4 billion across equity markets, interest rate securities, and foreign currency assets.
forward_outlook: HPS Transaction expected to close mid-2025 creating integrated private credit platform, while Preqin integration will incorporate private markets data into Aladdin technology platform. Issued euro-denominated notes due 2035 in April for general corporate purposes including refinancing maturing obligations. Amended credit facility increased capacity to $5.9 billion with extended maturity to March 2030.
uncertainty_profile: Contingent consideration liabilities total $4.4 billion including amounts related to GIP Transaction payable in shares subject to performance targets through December 2028. The company holds deferred carried interest subject to clawback provisions and maintains assets valued using significant unobservable inputs. Integration risks from recent acquisitions and HPS Transaction closing conditions remain subject to regulatory approvals.
